body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: HTTP clients via `@HttpExchange` interfaces.

✅ Annotate an interface — Boot builds the client

✅ `@GetExchange` / `@PostExchange` beat RestTemplate glue

✅ Tune timeouts/SSL with `spring.http.clients.*`

#SpringBoot4 #HttpExchange

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Native API versioning for MVC and WebFlux.

✅ Configure with `spring.mvc.apiversion.*`

✅ Version by header, path, or media type

✅ Plug in `ApiVersionResolver` for custom rules

#SpringBoot4 #ApiVersioning

🚀 Spring Boot 4: The codebase is modular — pick focused starters.

✅ Smaller jars instead of one giant Boot core

✅ Prefer `spring-boot-starter-*` for each tech

✅ Missing starter = missing auto-config (easy to miss)

#SpringBoot4 #Modular

🚀 Spring Boot 4: `spring-boot-starter-web` → `spring-boot-starter-webmvc`.

✅ Name matches the stack (MVC vs WebFlux)

✅ Pair with `spring-boot-starter-webmvc-test`

✅ Update BOM / parent to 4.x first

#SpringBoot4 #WebMVC
code:
<!-- ❌ Spring Boot 3 -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-web</artifactId>
</dependency>

<!-- ✅ Spring Boot 4 -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webmvc</artifactId>
</dependency>

<!-- tests -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-webmvc-test</artifactId>
  <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Jackson 3 is the default JSON library.

✅ Packages move: `com.fasterxml` → `tools.jackson`

✅ Prefer `JsonMapper` over a plain `ObjectMapper` bean

✅ Temporary escape hatch: `spring-boot-jackson2`

#SpringBoot4 #Jackson3

🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@MockBean` is gone — use `@MockitoBean`.

✅ Field mocks: `@MockitoBean` / `@MockitoSpyBean`

✅ Not for `@Configuration` classes anymore

✅ Prefer `@Import` test configs for shared mocks

#SpringBoot4 #Testing

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Prefer `RestTestClient` over `TestRestTemplate`.

✅ Fluent API aligned with `RestClient`

✅ Works with MockMvc or a real port

✅ Add `@AutoConfigureRestTestClient` when needed

#SpringBoot4 #RestTestClient

🚀 Spring Boot 4: New `spring-boot-starter-opentelemetry`.

✅ OTLP metrics + traces in one starter

✅ Auto-configures the OpenTelemetry SDK

✅ Drop Jaeger/Zipkin-only setup for OTLP collectors

#SpringBoot4 #OpenTelemetry
code:
<!-- pom.xml -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-opentelemetry</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.yml
management:
  otlp:
    tracing:
      endpoint: http://localhost:4318/v1/traces
    metrics:
      export:
        url: http://localhost:4318/v1/metrics
  tracing:
    sampling:
      probability: 1.0

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Portfolio-wide JSpecify null-safety.

✅ Clearer `@Nullable` / `@NonNull` contracts

✅ Better static analysis in IDEs

✅ Treat nullability warnings as real API signals

#SpringBoot4 #JSpecify

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Java 17 minimum, Java 25 supported.

✅ Stay on 17 if you must — migration still works

✅ New apps: prefer a current LTS (21+)

✅ GraalVM native-image needs 25+

#SpringBoot4 #Java
code:
<!-- pom.xml -->
<properties>
  <java.version>21</java.version>
  <spring-boot.version>4.0.7</spring-boot.version>
</properties>

<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>4.0.7</version>
</parent>

// Runtime check
System.out.println(Runtime.version()); // 17 … 25 OK

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Undertow support is removed.

✅ Servlet 6.1 baseline — Undertow isn’t there yet

✅ Stick with Tomcat or Jetty starters

✅ Don’t deploy Boot 4 to old servlet containers

#SpringBoot4 #Servlet

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Add `spring-boot-properties-migrator` while upgrading.

✅ Prints renamed/removed property diagnostics

✅ Temporarily remaps old keys at runtime

✅ Remove it once `application.yml` is clean

#SpringBoot4 #Migration
code:
<!-- temporary — runtime scope -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-properties-migrator</artifactId>
  <scope>runtime</scope>
</dependency>

// Startup log will flag things like:
// management.tracing.enabled → management.tracing.export.enabled
// spring.dao.exceptiontranslation.enabled
//   → spring.persistence.exceptiontranslation.enabled

// When quiet: delete the dependency.

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Tracing property rename to watch.

✅ `management.tracing.enabled` is gone

✅ Use `management.tracing.export.enabled`

✅ `@ConditionalOnEnabledTracing` → `…TracingExport`

#SpringBoot4 #Observability

🚀 Spring Boot 4: New `JmsClient` alongside `JmsTemplate`.

✅ Fluent, modern JMS API auto-configured

✅ `JmsTemplate` still works unchanged

✅ Prefer `JmsClient` for new messaging code

#SpringBoot4 #JMS

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Multiple `TaskDecorator` beans are supported.

✅ Boot wraps them in a `CompositeTaskDecorator`

✅ Order with `@Order` / `Ordered`

✅ Great for MDC + security context propagation

#SpringBoot4 #Async

🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@ConfigurationPropertiesSource` for external types.

✅ Metadata for types living in another module

✅ Keep annotation processor on the classpath

✅ IDE completion works across module boundaries

#SpringBoot4 #Config

🚀 Spring Boot 4: MongoDB health no longer needs Spring Data.

✅ Indicators live in `spring-boot-mongodb`

✅ Works with the MongoDB driver alone

✅ Use `spring-boot-starter-mongodb` when you need health

#SpringBoot4 #MongoDB
code:
<!-- Driver-only service still gets /actuator/health -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-mongodb</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-actuator</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.yml
spring:
  mongodb:
    uri: mongodb://localhost:27017/orders

management:
  endpoint:
    health:
      show-details: always

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Redis static master/replica (Lettuce).

✅ Set `spring.data.redis.masterreplica.nodes`

✅ Reads can go to replicas

✅ Lettuce-only — not Jedis

#SpringBoot4 #Redis

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Redis observability uses the Observation API.

✅ Metrics and spans from Redis commands

✅ Replaces older latency-recorder-only setup

✅ Pair with the OpenTelemetry starter

#SpringBoot4 #Redis
code:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-data-redis</artifactId>
</dependency>
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-opentelemetry</artifactId>
</dependency>

# Traces will include Redis command spans automatically
management:
  tracing:
    sampling:
      probability: 1.0

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Kotlin Serialization gets a first-class starter.

✅ `spring-boot-starter-kotlinx-serialization-json`

✅ Contributes a `Json` bean + HTTP converter

✅ Configure via `spring.kotlinx.serialization.json.*`

#SpringBoot4 #Kotlin

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Dedicated RestClient starter.

✅ `spring-boot-starter-restclient` for imperative HTTP

✅ WebClient → `spring-boot-starter-webclient`

✅ Unified `spring.http.clients.*` settings

#SpringBoot4 #RestClient
code:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-restclient</artifactId>
</dependency>

@Bean
RestClient catalogClient(RestClient.Builder builder) {
    return builder
        .baseUrl("https://catalog.internal")
        .build();
}

# application.yml
spring:
  http:
    clients:
      connect-timeout: 2s
      read-timeout: 5s

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Flyway/Liquibase now need their starters.

✅ Third-party jar alone is not enough

✅ Use `spring-boot-starter-flyway` / `liquibase`

✅ Matching `*-test` starters for test slices

#SpringBoot4 #Flyway
code:
<!-- ❌ Boot 3 style -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.flywaydb</groupId>
  <artifactId>flyway-core</artifactId>
</dependency>

<!-- ✅ Boot 4 -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-flyway</artifactId>
</dependency>

# application.yml
spring:
  flyway:
    locations: classpath:db/migration

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Test starters follow `spring-boot-starter-*-test`.

✅ Each tech has a matching test starter

✅ They pull `spring-boot-starter-test` transitively

✅ Example: Security tests need `…-security-test`

#SpringBoot4 #Testing
code:
<!-- ❌ Incomplete in Boot 4 -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.security</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-security-test</artifactId>
  <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>

<!-- ✅ Boot 4 -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-security-test</artifactId>
  <scope>test</scope>
</dependency>

@SpringBootTest
@WithMockUser(roles = "ADMIN")
class AdminApiTest { }

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Disable console logging with one property.

✅ `logging.console.enabled=false`

✅ Useful in containers that only scrape files/OTLP

✅ Keep file or remote appenders as needed

#SpringBoot4 #Logging
code:
# application-prod.yml
logging:
  console:
    enabled: false
  file:
    name: /var/log/app/application.log
  level:
    root: INFO
    com.example: DEBUG

# Local profile keeps console on (default true)

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: JDK `HttpClient` uses virtual threads when enabled.

✅ Set `spring.threads.virtual.enabled=true`

✅ Auto-configured HTTP clients opt in

✅ Great for high-concurrency blocking I/O

#SpringBoot4 #VirtualThreads

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Built on Jakarta EE 11 + Servlet 6.1.

✅ JPA 3.2, Validation 3.1, Servlet 6.1

✅ Hibernate ORM 7.x on the classpath

✅ Verify container / server Servlet level before deploy

#SpringBoot4 #JakartaEE

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Elasticsearch API key authentication.

✅ New `spring.elasticsearch.api-key` property

✅ Cleaner than embedding basic auth in the URI

✅ Rotate keys without changing client code

#SpringBoot4 #Elasticsearch

🚀 Spring Boot 4: SSL health shows `expiringChains`.

✅ No more `WILL_EXPIRE_SOON` status value

✅ Expiring certs stay `VALID` + listed in details

✅ Tune `management.health.ssl.certificate-validity-warning-threshold`

#SpringBoot4 #SSL

🚀 Spring Boot 4: DevTools live reload is off by default.

✅ Restarts still work; browser live reload is opt-in

✅ Enable only when you need it locally

✅ Avoid surprise websocket ports in shared envs

#SpringBoot4 #DevTools
code:
<!-- optional local dependency -->
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-devtools</artifactId>
  <optional>true</optional>
</dependency>

# application-local.yml
spring:
  devtools:
    livereload:
      enabled: true
    restart:
      enabled: true

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: `EnvironmentPostProcessor` package moved.

✅ New: `org.springframework.boot.EnvironmentPostProcessor`

✅ Old package kept temporarily (deprecated)

✅ Update `spring.factories` / `AutoConfiguration.imports`

#SpringBoot4 #Config

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Exception translation property renamed.

✅ `spring.dao.exceptiontranslation.enabled` →

✅ `spring.persistence.exceptiontranslation.enabled`

✅ Use the properties migrator to catch it

#SpringBoot4 #Migration
code:
# ❌ Boot 3
spring:
  dao:
    exceptiontranslation:
      enabled: true

# ✅ Boot 4
spring:
  persistence:
    exceptiontranslation:
      enabled: true

// Still translates DataAccessException → @Repository proxies

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@MeterTag` SpEL on `@Timed` / `@Counted`.

✅ Tag metric series from method args

✅ Auto-config wires a SpEL `ValueExpressionResolver`

✅ Keep cardinality under control

#SpringBoot4 #Micrometer

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Support for `@ObservationKeyValue`.

✅ Attach key/values to Micrometer Observations

✅ Cleaner than manual `observation.highCardinalityKeyValue`

✅ Works with the OTLP starter export path

#SpringBoot4 #Observability

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Gradle 9 is supported.

✅ Build with Gradle 9 or 8.14+

✅ Keep the Boot Gradle plugin in sync with Boot 4

✅ Good moment to refresh toolchain + JVM args

#SpringBoot4 #Gradle

🚀 Spring Boot 4: AWS ECS is a recognized `CloudPlatform`.

✅ Detects Elastic Container Service runtime

✅ Aligns cloud property / actuator behavior

✅ Same idea as k8s / Cloud Foundry detection

#SpringBoot4 #AWS

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Control MongoDB `BigDecimal` storage.

✅ `spring.data.mongodb.representation.big-decimal`

✅ Avoid silent precision surprises

✅ Pick decimal128 vs string explicitly

#SpringBoot4 #MongoDB

🚀 Spring Boot 4: `@ServiceConnection` + MongoDB Atlas Local.

✅ Testcontainers `MongoDBAtlasLocalContainer` supported

✅ No hardcoded localhost ports in tests

✅ Boot wires connection details automatically

#SpringBoot4 #Testcontainers

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Prefer `JsonMapper` beans over `ObjectMapper`.

✅ Jackson 3 format-specific mappers

✅ Replacing JSON config? Define `JsonMapper`

✅ XML? Define `XmlMapper`

#SpringBoot4 #Jackson3

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Fully executable uber-jar launch scripts are gone.

✅ `java -jar app.jar` is the supported path

✅ Unix init-script packaging removed

✅ Use buildpacks, systemd, or Gradle application plugin

#SpringBoot4 #Packaging

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Upgrade path tip — go 3.5 → 4.0.

✅ Land on latest 3.5.x first

✅ Kill 3.x deprecations before the jump

✅ Then bump parent/BOM to 4.0.x and fix starters

#SpringBoot4 #Migration
code:
<!-- Step 1: latest 3.5.x, fix deprecations -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>3.5.16</version>
</parent>

<!-- Step 2: Boot 4 -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>4.0.7</version>
</parent>

<!-- Step 3: properties-migrator (temporary) + starter renames -->

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: HTTP Service Clients + `RestClient` under one roof.

✅ Interfaces for declarative calls

✅ `RestClient` for ad-hoc calls

✅ Shared `spring.http.clients` timeouts/SSL bundles

#SpringBoot4 #HttpExchange

🚀 Spring Boot 4: API versioning beans for advanced control.

✅ Provide `ApiVersionResolver`

✅ Optional `ApiVersionParser`

✅ `ApiVersionDeprecationHandler` for sunsetting

#SpringBoot4 #ApiVersioning

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Spring Security 7 comes along for the ride.

✅ Security filter chain DSL stays familiar

✅ Stronger crypto defaults — review customizers

✅ Use `spring-boot-starter-security` + `…-security-test`

#SpringBoot4 #SpringSecurity

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Tomcat static resource cache size is configurable.

✅ `server.tomcat.resource.cache-max-size`

✅ Tune when serving many static assets

✅ Defaults may surprise large SPAs

#SpringBoot4 #Tomcat
code:
# application.yml
server:
  tomcat:
    resource:
      cache-max-size: 10MB
  compression:
    enabled: true
    mime-types: text/html,text/xml,text/plain,application/json

spring:
  web:
    resources:
      chain:
        cache: true

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Mark development tools in the jar manifest.

✅ `Spring-Boot-Jar-Type: development-tool`

✅ Keeps those deps out of production uber jars

✅ Useful for internal developer utilities

#SpringBoot4 #Packaging

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Milestones publish to Maven Central.

✅ No extra `repo.spring.io` for 4.x milestones

✅ Easier CI trials of M/RC builds

✅ Still pin versions explicitly in experiments

#SpringBoot4 #Maven
code:
<!-- try a milestone without custom repositories -->
<parent>
  <groupId>org.springframework.boot</groupId>
  <artifactId>spring-boot-starter-parent</artifactId>
  <version>4.1.0-M2</version>
</parent>

// Gradle: mavenCentral() is enough for Boot 4 milestones
repositories {
  mavenCentral()
}

body:
🚀 Spring Boot 4: Auto-config classes are no longer public API.

✅ Public members removed from auto-config types

✅ Don’t `@Import` auto-config classes directly

✅ Use starters + `AutoConfiguration.imports` only

#SpringBoot4 #AutoConfig

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Session Hazelcast/MongoDB starters removed.

✅ Ownership moved to Hazelcast / MongoDB teams

✅ Use their Spring Session modules directly

✅ Redis + JDBC session starters remain in Boot

#SpringBoot4 #Session

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Spock integration removed (Groovy 5).

✅ Spock isn’t ready for Groovy 5 yet

✅ Prefer JUnit 5 + AssertJ for Boot 4 tests

✅ Keep Spock suites on Boot 3.5 until Spock catches up

#SpringBoot4 #Testing

🚀 Spring Boot 4: Built on Spring Framework 7 — foundations matter.

✅ HTTP Service Clients + API versioning land in Boot

✅ JSpecify null-safety across the portfolio

✅ Plan starter renames + Jackson 3 before you upgrade

#SpringBoot4 #SpringFramework7
